disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A raised scalp lump has grown rapidly over about 6 weeks from a rough patch to a firm crusted nodule
trigger_question_examples: How long has the lump been there?
Has it changed or grown recently?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started as a rough patch about six weeks ago and grew quite fast into a firm raised lump with a thick rough crust on top.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The lesion is on a chronically sun-exposed bald scalp with surrounding rough sun-damaged skin
trigger_question_examples: Has that area had a lot of sun over the years?
Do you have other rough or scaly spots on your scalp?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My head's been bald for years and had loads of sun. I've got a few rough scaly sun spots up there too.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Active bleeding from the ulcerated centre of the lesion after minor trauma, which the patient cannot control
trigger_question_examples: Can you stop the bleeding with pressure?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes it's still oozing blood from the raw middle. I've soaked a towel and I can't get a good press on the top of my own head.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: New constant deep throbbing pain localised to the lesion
trigger_question_examples: What does the pain feel like?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's a deep constant throb right at the lump, and it won't settle.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Patient lives alone and is functionally unable to visualise or apply effective pressure to the top of the scalp
trigger_question_examples: Is anyone with you who can help?
Can you reach and press on the spot yourself?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I live on my own. I can't see the top of my head and I can't press on it properly by myself.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No feeling of faintness, dizziness, or light-headedness reported at present
trigger_question_examples: Do you feel faint, dizzy or light-headed?
Have you felt like you might pass out?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I don't feel faint or dizzy — just frustrated and sore.
